zh.lifeSummary: 當Guillaume IV DE SAINTE MAURE於1258年出生在法國i萊-德-福朗斯瓦爾-德-馬內沙吉莫-德-福瑟斯時，他的父親Guillaume III de Sainte Maure de Marsillac29歲，母親Jeanne de RANCON15歲。他和Isabeau D'AMBOISE至少有2個女兒。他去世於1300年享年42歲。

nameMeanings.name: DE
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: SAINTE
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant of   Sain   1, a surname of German origin.
French: probably a variant of Saïn (see Sain 6).
West African (Gambia): variant of the Serer name Sène (see Sene 2).

variants.name: Sain
meanings: Americanized form of German Zirn, which is a variant of Zürn (see Zurn ). Compare Saine , Sane , and Sayne .

Croatian and Serbian (Šain): metonymic occupational name for a hawker or a nickname from ša(h)in ‘falcon, hawk (trained for hunting)’, a word of Turkish and ultimately Persian origin (see Sahin ). Compare Shain and Shine .

Americanized form of Slovenian and Croatian Šajn: nickname from šajn, an obsolete loanword from German meaning ‘shine’, or a Slavicized form of German Schein . As a Croatian surname it may also be a variant of Šain (see 2 above). Compare Shain and Shine .

variants.name: Sene
meanings: French (Sené and Séné): nickname from Old French sené ‘wise, sensible’.

West African (Senegal; Sène): name found among the Serer people, of unexplained etymology. The same name forms part of the name of Roog Seen, the supreme deity in the indigenous Serer religion. Compare Saine 3.

nameMeanings.name: MAURE
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Ernesto, Ramon, Adela, Carlos, Eladio, Emilia, Estrella, Horacio, Jorge, Jose, Juan, Mario.  Italian Alessandra, Angelo, Cecilio, Cosimo, Gianfranco, Lorenzo, Mauro.
Catalan: from the personal name Mauri, Latin Maurus (see Mauro ).
Catalan and French (Occitan): probably from the personal name Malric (ancient Germanic Almaricus).

variants.name: Mauro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Sal, Domenic, Giuseppe, Antonio, Carmine, Carmelo, Domenico, Gaetano, Nino, Pasquale.

Italian (mainly southern): from the personal name Mauro, derived from the Latin personal and ethnic name Maurus, borne by various early Christian saints and meaning ‘Moor, North African’. Compare Moore and Moro .

nameMeanings.name: Guillaume
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Germanic. See William .

variants.name: William
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: IV
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Guillaume IV DE SAINTE MAURE was born in 1258, in Saint-Maur-des-Fossés, Val-de-Marne, Île-de-France, France, his father, Guillaume III de Sainte Maure de Marsillac, was 29 and his mother, Jeanne de RANCON, was 15. He had at least 2 daughters with Isabeau D'AMBOISE. He died in 1300, at the age of 42.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Guillaume IV DE SAINTE MAURE est né en 1258, à Saint-Maur-des-Fossés, Val-de-Marne, Île-de-France, France, son père, Guillaume III de Sainte Maure de Marsillac, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Jeanne de RANCON, avait 15 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 filles avec Isabeau D'AMBOISE. Il est décédé en 1300, à l’âge de 42 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Guillaume IV DE SAINTE MAURE 1258 in Saint-Maur-des-Fossés, Val-de-Marne, Île-de-France, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Guillaume III de Sainte Maure de Marsillac 29 und seine Mutter Jeanne de RANCON 15. Er hatte mit Isabeau D'AMBOISE mindestens 2 Töchter. Er starb 1300 im Alter von 42 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Guillaume IV DE SAINTE MAURE è nato il nel 1258 nel Saint-Maur-des-Fossés, Val-de-Marne, Île-de-France, Francia, il suo padre, Guillaume III de Sainte Maure de Marsillac, aveva 29 anni e il suo madre, Jeanne de RANCON, aveva 15 anni. Lui avuto almeno 2 figlie con Isabeau D'AMBOISE. Morì nel 1300, all'età di 42 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Guillaume IV DE SAINTE MAURE(이)가 1258년 프랑스 i레-데-프랜스 발-데-마니 세인트-모-데스-포스e스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Guillaume III de Sainte Maure de Marsillac(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Jeanne de RANCON(은)는 15세였다. 그는 Isabeau D'AMBOISE(와)과 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1300년 42세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Guillaume IV DE SAINTE MAURE nasceu em 1258, em Saint-Maur-des-Fossés, Val-de-Marne, Île-de-France, França, seu pai, Guillaume III de Sainte Maure de Marsillac, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Jeanne de RANCON, tinha 15 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhas com Isabeau D'AMBOISE. Ele faleceu em 1300, com 42 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Guillaume IV DE SAINTE MAURE родился в 1258 г. в местности сеинт-маур-дес-фоссeс, вал-де-марн, iле-де-фрэнс, Франция, его отцу по имени Guillaume III de Sainte Maure de Marsillac было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Jeanne de RANCON, 15. У него было по крайней мере 2 дочери от Isabeau D'AMBOISE. Он умер в 1300 г. в возрасте 42 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Guillaume IV DE SAINTE MAURE nació en 1258, en Saint-Maur-des-Fossés, Val-de-Marne, Île-de-France, Francia, su papá, Guillaume III de Sainte Maure de Marsillac, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Jeanne de RANCON, tenía 15 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijas con Isabeau D'AMBOISE. Murió en 1300, a la edad de 42 años.
